name: Rocky Linux
description: Rocky Linux is a community-supported, bug-for-bug compatible replacement for Red Hat Enterprise Linux (RHEL), created after CentOS Stream pivoted away from being a downstream rebuild. It targets enterprises, production servers, and organisations that need long-term ABI stability and a predictable, well-tested release cadence. Each major version receives 10 years of support. DNF and the RPM ecosystem are mature and well-understood by administrators worldwide. Not suited for desktop use, but arguably the best free RHEL alternative for server workloads.
